rationale: In the "Relationship to prior Research" section, the cited paper is discussed with a specific empirical pattern rather than being only name-checked. The authors use Keller et al. (2003), alongside an opposing mood-framing result, to argue that framing effects are not simply mood effects and that prior affect-by-frame findings are mixed. This helps position their construal-level account and motivates their tests ruling out mood-based explanations.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Keller, Lipkus, and Rimer (2003) as prior evidence of an affect x framing interaction (positive mood favoring loss frames, negative mood favoring gain frames). It then leverages that evidence to frame the literature as mixed and to justify a distinct construal-level mechanism rather than a mood-proxy account.
